column2:
Father [V2]
(with Kanye West)
[prod. Kanye West]
column4: Seen on an updated 'Bully' tracklist posted by Ye on March 25, 2026 and features "No AI". Later previewed at the Los Angeles 'Bully' listening party. Samples "Heavenly Father, You've Been Good" by Johnnie Frierson and a clip of a James Brown interview on CNN from 1998.

column2: Comments
column4: Spectators can now add comments. Use this feature if you believe some info is wrong or missing, or to get inactive links reuploaded. For further questions join Travis Scott Hub, the invite can be found in 'Links'. Abuse or the resolving of comments by non-editors will result in removal of the feature.

column2:
A Man [Demo 2]
[prod. Southside, Max Levin & Jake One]
column4: ProTools session snippet for a demo of 'A Man'. Features about a minute of vocals, the entire session includes over 100 vocal takes. Sounds near-final. Snippet leaked April 19, 2026.

column2:

[prod. Vinylz]
column4: Solo Travis version without Quavo, made during sessions for 'Astroworld'. Missing some adlibs. Snippet leaked April 19, 2026.

column2: Fire Drill
column4: Made on February 21, 2017; contains about 2 minutes of vocals in the bounced file, and over 300 audio files in the session folder. Snippet leaked April 19, 2026.

column2: Untitled 1
column4: ProTools session snippet with about 45 seconds of vocals on the bounced file, according to the seller; the audio files include more vocals, some of them being mumble. Recorded on March 17, 2017. Snippet leaked April 19, 2026.

column2: Untitled 2
column4: ProTools session snippet to a mumble freestyle song, recorded on February 20, 2017. The bounced file has around 2:30 minutes of vocals. Snippet leaked April 19, 2026.

column2: Untitled 3
column4: ProTools session snippet with about 15 seconds of vocals in the bounced file, the other audio files include some more mumble vocals. Recorded on February 20, 2017. Snippet leaked April 19, 2026.

column2:

(feat. Travis Scott & Young Thug)
[prod. Metro Boomin & Allen Ritter]
column4:
OG Filename: Trance V1c Mix_Master 6.6.22
Version dated three days before 'Demo 3'. Utilises an earlier mix and weaker vocals effects on Drake's vocals; also, Travis' adlib at 2:25 appears to be louder than on the following version. Leaked April 5, 2026.

column2:
✨ 21 Savage - née-nah [Demo]
(ref. Dougie F; with Travis Scott & Metro Boomin)
[prod. Metro Boomin]
column4:
OG Filename: dougie x 21TS - hook
Early, presumably untitled version with a hook reference performed by Dougie F on an alternate instrumental, likely meant for 21 Savage to record over; the first hook comes in during Travis' verse. Has a few different Travis' bars at the end of his verse, an additional unheard verse from 21, an open section at the end and overall an early mix. Leaked April 5, 2026.

column2:
Father [Demo 1]
(with Kanye West)
[prod. Kanye West, Travis Scott, Jahaan Sweet & 2 Vicious]
column4: Early version seemingly fully pitched down, including the sample; missing the James Brown's interview sample. Previewed during a commercial for Beats by Dre on December 19, 2025. Made in November in Tokyo, after Travis had brought out Ye at his concert. Later heard in a brief BTS for the music video on March 29, 2026, and during a making of posted to Instagram on April 1, 2026.

column2:
✨ Playboi Carti - Rockstar Made [Demo]
(feat. Travis Scott)
[prod. F1lthy & Jonah Abraham]
column4: A tweet from Carti implied that Travis recorded a verse for 'Whole Lotta Red', but it didn't appear on the tracklist. In 2021, streamer Adin Ross stated that Travis originally appeared on "Rockstar Made"; he later played a snippet of the song during a livestream in the early hours of April 13, 2023, prompting an angry phonecall from Travis's manager, Young Larry David. Features additional vocals, Travis performs some form of intro, several adlibs, and a verse. Highly desired song before it leaked on January 27, 2026.

column2:
Don Toliver - Call Back [Demo 2]
[prod. 206Derek & Roark Bailey]
column4: Finished version with Don's second verse. Lacks the mix and master from the releasing version, and the additional production that's allegedly coming from Don, and features an alternate second verse. Part of a June copy of 'JackBoys 2' but ended up being replaced by "No Comments". Snippet leaked January 23, 2026.

column2: New '🥇Wanted' sheet
column4: The Tracker Editorial team has decided to add a 'Wanted' sheet which includes songs that are fairly desired by the community but might not belong in 'Grails'. It only includes songs that are confirmed to exist, which is why you won't find songs such as "All Your Fault (Tom Cruise)", "Moses" or "4th Dimension" on it.

⭐ Kanye West - Pressure
(feat. Travis Scott)
[prod. Havoc]
column4:
OG Filename*: trav uzi pressure
Kanye throwaway that was worked on in 2014-15, originally starting out as "Get Off Me"; this final known version features Travis and was made during 'SWISH' sessions (see the Kanye tracker for information on other versions). Unofficially known as "Can U Be", another version had a snippet leak through Music Mafia in mid-2017. This version was teased on Instagram Live by Che Pope, on October 22, 2020. The intro samples the 2012 film 'Silver Linings Playbook', while the synths are sampled from "Haunted", from the 'Maniac' soundtrack. Uzi was present in the studio, but didn't record for this song. Leaked May 26, 2024.

column2:
Marietta
[prod. Metro Boomin, Allen Ritter & Travis Scott]
column4:
OG Filename*: METRO_ALLEN_TRAVIS NEW IDEA 1
Throwaway idea from the same session as "Wine and Hennessy", produced by Metro Boomin and Allen Ritter; never actually given an official title, with the session files being titled "METRO_ALLEN_TRAVIS NEW IDEA 1". The full bounce is an hour long, and contains three separate freestyles from Travis; time inbetween these freestyles is spent working on the instrumental. The third freestyle from the session leaked June 28, 2019. The full bounce leaked May 26, 2024. Two files are linked; one file is muted in places and is technically the correct bounce, while the other is unmuted and has an extended freestyle as a result. The unmuted version features Travis and Metro talking about Justin Bieber recording for "Drunk", Metro talking about working with 21 Savage, and Travis criticising the sequencing of Meek Mill's 'Dreams Worth More Than Money, among many other things.

[prod. Metro Boomin & Allen Ritter]
(AKA: Wine and Hennesy)
column4:
OG Filename: WINE AND HENNESY IDEA
Features Travis laying down reference vocals for about ten minutes over an evolving beat produced by Metro Boomin and Allen Ritter. Privately sold by Music Mafia and then previewed via an edited snippet under the name "All The Time"; it was labelled as featuring The Weeknd, but this was an intentional false claim to catch scammers. Sometimes shortened to "Hennessy" before the title was confirmed; "WINE AND HENNESY IDEA" was the original filename (with the misspelling). The song wasn't bounced, and Music Mafia didn't even attempt it, so it leaked without the vocal effects that it was intended to have on February 6, 2018. An inaccurate recreation surfaced in November 2020. A completely accurate bounce leaked September 7, 2022.
